LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DR. ALVIN TANG AND DR. GORTON LEE'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED NO EARLIER THAN DECEMBER 1, 2011 IF EITHER DEFENDANT HAS NOT BEEN PROPERLY SERVED BY THAT DATE. THE POLICY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA THAT LAWSUITS SHOULD BE DECIDED ON THEIR MERITS, THE LACK OF APPARENT PREJUDICE TO THE DEFENDANTS, AND THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF IS A SELF-REPRESENTED LITIGANT FAVOR GIVING PLAINTIFF ONE FINAL OPPORTUNITY TO PURSUE THIS ACTION IN AN EXPEDITIOUS MANNER. THERE IS NO INDICATION IN THE RECORDS REVIEWED BY THE COURT THAT EITHER DR. TANG OR DR. LEE HAS BEEN PROPERLY SERVED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

Defendants Dr. Alvintang And Dr. Gorton Lee'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
Off Calendar Oct-24-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - BOLLA GODO, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. WILLAIM J. EDGAR OF BUTY & CURLIANO LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 267-3000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - THE MATTER CALLED TWICE. ON THE SECOND CALL OF THE MATTER DUE TO THE LATE APPEARANCE OF MR. EDGAR AND MR. GODO HAVE ALREADY LEFT THE COURT ROOM, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendants Dr. Alvin Tang and Dr. Gorton Lee's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute is Denied without prejudice to being refiled no earlier than December 1, 2011 if either defendant has not been properly served by that date. The policy of the State of California that lawsuits should be decided on their merits, the lack of apparent prejudice to the defendants, and the fact that plaintiff is a self-represented litigant favor giving plaintiff one final opportunity to pursue this action in an expeditious manner. There is no indication in the records reviewed by the court that either Dr. Tang or Dr. Lee has been properly served. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
